column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: I can't believe she said yes !

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: I know. Normally I'd have to spend weeks laying down the leg work to land a beautiful baby like that.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: You are so lucky that Zach didn't get there first.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: You mean Mr. Bad Ties ? I wasn't worried about him. He's one of those guys who has a wardrobe but no personality. Saundra doesn't want that. She wants someone who can MAKE her laugh. Not someone she can laugh AT.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Hand me the 3/8ths.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Here. Am I ever going to get to run the controls for one these capers ?

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: When I think you're ready.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: When will that be ?

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: When you are ready.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Look, you're going to have to start trusting me sometime. I am a trustworthy person.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Trust is earned not given away.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: RESPECT is earned not given away, trust is given away to anybody.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Not down here. I'll start trusting you when I feel there the time is right. It's not personal, it's just survival.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: I've been working with you for four years, don't you think that's worthy of at least one turn at the controls ?

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: No.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: I made my bones on the street, you know. I did Riglioni in his own house. I was made in the Family, okay.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: And who bailed you out when a hit was put on you, you sorry sack of shit. Who put you up ? Who gave you a job ?

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: You did.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Do you know much about icebergs, Dad?

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Do I? I saw an iceberg once. They were hauling it down to Texas for drinking water, only they didn't count on an elephant being frozen inside. The woolly kind. A mammoth.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Dad!

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: What?

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: I'm trying to make a metaphor here.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Then you shouldn't have started with a question. Because people want to answer questions. You should have started with, The thing about icebergs is...

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: The thing about icebergs is you only see 10 percent of them. The other 90 percent is below the water where you can't see it. And that's what it is with you Dad. I'm only seeing this little bit that sticks above the water.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: What, you're seeing down to my nose? My chin?

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: I have no idea who you are because you have never told me a single fact.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: I've told you a thousand facts. That's all I do, Will. I tell stories.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: You think I'm fake.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Only on the surface. But that's all I've ever seen.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Dad, I'm about to have a kid of my own here. It would kill me if he went through his whole life never understanding me.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: It would kill you, huh?

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: What do you want, Will? Who do you want me to be?

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Yourself. Good, bad, everything. Just show me who you are for once.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: I have been nothing but myself since the day I was born. And if you can't see that, it's your failing, not mine.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Your father decided he needed to have an office, and it wouldn't do to have it in the house. You'll know better than me what's important.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: What is it?

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: It was during the war. Your father went missing. They thought he was dead.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: That really happened?

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Not everything your father says is a complete fabrication.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Two drinks and I start swinging from the rooftops. Tell me, Vicki. There's something I'm very curious about. What took you down to Corto Maltese?

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: ... I guess I needed a change.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: You were one of the most successful magazine photographers in the city. Everyone wanted you.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Have you ever been to Corto Maltese?

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Not since the shooting started.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: We went there once when I was little. I played on the beach. And at nights -- they had a band -- I danced with my father on the hotel patio. That was Corto Maltese. When the war broke out I had to go back. And I promised myself that this time... I wouldn't look away.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: What did you see?

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: ... Terror.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: There's terror everywhere. Some types are just more -- familiar than others.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: I'm sorry. I didn't mean to -- I know it all seems a million miles away, out here on the water, with all this --

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Insulation?

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Bruce, really, when I say these things I don't mean to criticize you.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: I think you see things very clearly.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: I'm happy to talk about something else. I don't want to be depressing.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Do you assume that if I know you better I won't like you as much?

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: I'm sorry, Bruce, I have to ask. Are you like this with the other women you know? -- Because I just can't seem to get a handle on this conversation.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Vicki, if I say anything cryptic, or... ambiguous, I think you should put the most flattering possible interpretation on it. Because even if it doesn't sound that way... that's how I'll mean it.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: -- but it's not fair. I'm half drunk and you're not even --

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Would you like me to take you home?

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: God. You would. Come on, Bruce. I just want to get two drinks in you. As an experiment.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Maybe we should just kiss.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: ... We could try that.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: What do you do, Tyler?

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: What do you want me to do?

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: I mean -- for a living.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Why? So you can say, Oh, that's what you do. -- And be a smug little shit about it?

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: You have a kind of sick desperation in your laugh.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: We have the same briefcase.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Open it.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Soap -- the yardstick of civilization. I make and sell soap...

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: If you were to add nitric acid to the soap-making process, one would get nitroglycerin. With enough soap, one could blow up the world, if one were so inclined.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Tyler, you are by far the most interesting single-serving friend I've ever met.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: You see, when you travel, everything is small, self-contained--

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: The spork. I get it. You're very clever.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Thank you.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: How's that working out for you?

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: What?

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Being clever.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Well, uh... great.

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
column7: Keep it up, then. Keep it right up.

Long ago the Men of the Mountain swore an oath to the last King of Condor - to come to his aid, to fight, but when the time came, when Condor's need was dire, they fled ... Vanishing into the darkness of the mountain ... And so Isildur cursed them - never to rest until they had fulfilled their pledge. . . .

column3: leyou LI, xiaojing YANG, yijie LIU
